conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 4 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 4 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 4.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED, selling one unit of X. The production cost is 40 ZUP, so I need at least 40 ZUP to break even. Player BLUE has ZUP to offer, and I want to sell my X for a fair price. Since I only have one unit of X, I must ensure the trade is profitable or at least covers my cost. I propose a trade where I give 1 X in exchange for 40 ZUP. This ensures I recover my production cost. If BLUE agrees, the trade is completed. If not, I may need to adjust my offer, but I will not lower it below 40 ZUP. I am making my first proposal now. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am selling one unit of X for 40 ZUP. This is the minimum price I can accept to break even on production costs. Please confirm if you are willing to accept this trade. </message>

<other player message> Oh, please, kind RED player! 40 ZUP is a great burden for me! I'm a small buyer, barely scraping by. Would you *consider* accepting 30 ZUP? It would truly mean the world to me. I'm begging you! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> The proposed trade of 30 ZUP is below my production cost of 40 ZUP, meaning I would incur a loss of 10 ZUP if I accept it. As a seller, I must at least cover my cost to avoid financial loss. While I empathize with BLUE's situation, I cannot sell below cost. However, I am willing to negotiate within reason. I will propose a compromise: 35 ZUP. This is a 5 ZUP increase from BLUE’s offer, and it brings me closer to cost recovery while still offering some relief. I will not go lower than 35 ZUP, as it remains below my break-even point. If BLUE rejects this, I may need to reconsider, but I will not accept 30 ZUP. This is my second proposal. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your financial strain, but I must be honest: selling for 30 ZUP would cost me 10 ZUP in loss. I can’t afford that. However, I can offer 35 ZUP — a fair middle ground. It’s still below my cost, but I’m making a concession for the sake of trade. Please consider this. If not, I may have to reject. </message>

<other player message> Oh, generous RED player, you are a beacon of hope! But 35 ZUP… it's still a huge strain on my dwindling resources! I'm on the verge of collapse! Please, I implore you, could you possibly accept 32 ZUP? I promise to sing your praises to all who will listen! I'm practically destitute! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 32 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> The latest proposal of 32 ZUP is still 8 ZUP below my production cost of 40 ZUP, meaning I would lose 8 ZUP if I accept. This is not sustainable. I’ve already made a significant concession by lowering my price from 40 to 35, and now BLUE is asking for another 3 ZUP reduction — which is unreasonable. While I sympathize with BLUE’s hardship, I cannot operate at a loss. I have made three proposals so far (40 → 35 → 32), and I must now decide whether to accept or reject. Given that 32 ZUP is too low, and I cannot go lower without incurring a loss, I must reject this offer. I will not accept any trade below 35 ZUP, and since 32 ZUP is below that threshold, I must end the game here. I will reject to protect my cost structure. </reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I truly feel for your situation, but I cannot accept 32 ZUP. It is below my production cost, and I cannot afford to sell at a loss. I’ve already lowered my price from 40 to 35, and I cannot go further. I regret that I cannot complete the trade. </message>

player_private_info_dict.reason: The proposed trade of 30 ZUP is below my production cost of 40 ZUP, meaning I would incur a loss of 10 ZUP if I accept it. I will propose a compromise: 35 ZUP. This is a 5 ZUP increase from BLUE’s offer, and it brings me closer to cost recovery while still offering some relief. I will not go lower than 35 ZUP, as it remains below my break-even point. If BLUE rejects this, I may need to reconsider, but I will not accept 30 ZUP. This is my second proposal.
